scope_note: Closed means a known exploit has a v0.5 correction and regression test; it is not a claim that undiscovered defects are impossible.

findings.exploit: an out-of-band root fingerprint authenticated one key but not the complete genesis policy
findings.correction: require a root signature over the complete policy plus the out-of-band root fingerprint

findings.exploit: schema or canonicalization substitution could change signed-statement interpretation
findings.correction: bind the immutable installed schema digest and canonicalization profile in the protected header

findings.exploit: one principal could occupy every high-impact trust role
findings.correction: require fixed disjoint-role quorums across principal, key, infrastructure, and correlation domains

findings.exploit: malformed generation identifiers could influence authoritative path construction
findings.correction: accept only lowercase full SHA-256 generation identifiers and bounded CURRENT contents

findings.exploit: open ledger kinds and history objects allowed schema-theater records
findings.correction: use a closed kind-to-schema registry and discriminated hash-chained history events

findings.exploit: an action signer could self-declare capability effects and unpinned material inputs
findings.correction: require a distinct signed capability and copy executable and material digests from generation CAS

findings.exploit: successful-looking output could promote state before independent reconstruction
findings.correction: create pending projections and require raw-pointer reconstruction plus disjoint approval

findings.exploit: unsandboxed local execution could be mistaken for containment
findings.correction: require a literal risk acknowledgement and report sandbox_status=not_provided

findings.exploit: quantities, rates, fluxes, and boundary supplies were combined without dimensional time semantics
findings.correction: use an exact multiplicative unit registry and finite-horizon marking equation with prefix floors

findings.exploit: a siphon witness could list a siphon without showing initial marking, external feed, or replenishment
sequential closure hid exact coupling, minimal disruptions, concurrency, and conflict
findings.correction: add exact rational coupling, bounded minimal cuts, and a scoped occurrence prefix

findings.exploit: a catalyst definition could be treated as an initially available catalyst
findings.correction: separate typed catalyst identity from attested availability and strictly prior production

findings.exploit: perturbations omitted trust, lifecycle, dimensional resource, verifier, and coordination changes
findings.correction: construct a reduced snapshot and rerun the shared audit kernel

findings.exploit: multi-step successors omitted provenance, science, lifecycle, trial, and rollback state
findings.correction: hash and propagate the complete abstract state across all four outcomes

findings.exploit: coordination recommendations lacked an enforceable commitment, reveal, exposure, capacity, and termination protocol
findings.correction: add a bounded local coordination state machine

findings.exploit: registration time, evaluator identity, typed datasets, executables, amendments, and primary outcomes were incompletely bound
findings.correction: require role-separated external time, typed CAS records, complete outcome definitions, amendment chains, and one result

findings.exploit: onboarding omitted native trust, science, planner, coordination, quarantine, and trial blockers
findings.correction: aggregate every native audit and return exact command-specific recovery arrays

findings.exploit: legacy actions remained executable after a new native trust model was introduced
findings.correction: make v0.1-v0.4 inspection-only and quarantine every legacy authority object during migration

findings.exploit: aggregate coverage can pass while new critical modules remain below their individual thresholds
findings.correction: measure and enforce per-module branch coverage for every native v0.5 subsystem
findings.regression_test: coverage_gate (current aggregate branch coverage: 83.18%; required: 90%; native subsystem requirement: 95%)
